label: Organization & Formatting
description: All formatting guidelines are followed; YAML is correctly filled out.

label: Organization & Formatting
description: Most formatting guidelines are followed; YAML is correctly filled out.

label: Organization & Formatting
description: Several or all formatting guidelines not followed; YAML contains elements that aren't updated from the template; template file wasn't used.

label: Data Documentation
description: The README file included in the template was updated with thoughtful and accurate information about the data.

label: Data Documentation
description: The README file included in the template was updated, but some of the information was missing or inaccurate.

label: Data Documentation
description: The README file included in the template was not updated, missing, or most of the information in it was inaccurate.

label: Strategy
description: A specific, numbered strategy is written before implementation; it correctly identifies the key judgment calls in the data (e.g. distinguishing countries from regions/subtotals) and describes how the data will be reshaped and cleaned.

label: Strategy
description: A strategy is present and numbered, but is vague in places or misses one of the key judgment calls.

label: Strategy
description: Strategy is missing, generic/copied from the assignment prompt, or clearly written after the cleaning code rather than guiding it.

label: Data Cleaning
description: The resulting "clean" data frame is exactly (or very close to) the desired final state.

label: Data Cleaning
description: The resulting "clean" data frame has one or two significant errors that prevent it from being in the desired final state.

label: Data Cleaning
description: Multiple errors prevent the resulting "clean" data frame from being in the desired final state.

label: Verification
description: Runs and reports on multiple concrete, specific checks (e.g. row/shape counts, a duplicate check, an NA audit, an independent reconciliation against totals reported in the source file, manual spot-checks) with real output shown; clearly documents at least one place where Claude's first attempt was wrong and how it was caught and corrected. The exported transcript corroborates that the student -- not just the agent -- did the verifying and reasoning.

label: Verification
description: Runs some real checks, but they are generic (e.g. only `head()`/`glimpse()`) or the write-up asserts correctness without independent evidence. The transcript suggests verification was mostly outsourced to the agent (e.g. "please verify this is correct") rather than the student directly checking.

label: Verification
description: No real verification beyond visually inspecting the output. Claims the data was "double checked" or "verified" with no supporting checks in the report or evidence in the transcript.

label: Technical & Process
description: All code runs without errors; the html file renders from the qmd; the exported Claude Code transcript is included; all files are included in the submitted .zip file.

label: Technical & Process
description: Code has only one or two errors, otherwise runs; transcript included but incomplete, or a minor file is missing from the submitted .zip.

label: Technical & Process
description: Code has multiple errors; html file cannot be rendered without significant changes; transcript is missing; submitted .zip is missing components necessary to reproduce the analysis.
